goal: Build memory layout and save data structures — WRAM/HRAM/SRAM layout, party struct, box struct, trainer card, options
abstract_tasks: Define Rust structs mirroring PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48 and BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=32 byte layouts with all fields (species, item, moves, PP, happiness, DVs, StatExp, level, status, HP, stats, caught data)
Implement WRAM battle union layout as Rust enums/unions: BattleState containing active battle fields (substatus1-5 bytes, stat level arrays, screen flags, weather, damage trackers, turn counters)
Define HRAM equivalents as Rust fields: RNG state (hRandomAdd/hRandomSub), math registers (hMultiplicand/hMultiplier/hProduct), hBattleTurn, hJoyPressed

Build SRAM save structure: sOptions (text speed, battle style, sound, frame), sPlayerData (name, ID, money, badges), sPartyMon array, sPokemonBoxes (14 boxes x 20 Pokemon), sMysteryGiftData, sBattleTowerData
Implement wEventFlags as a 1400+ bit array with named accessors for all key story events (EVENT_GOT_A_POKEMON_FROM_ELM, EVENT_BEAT_ELITE_FOUR, etc.)
Define player data fields: wPlayerName (7 chars), wPlayerID (u16), wMoney (3-byte BCD), wJohtoBadges/wKantoBadges (u8 each as bit arrays)
Implement caught data packing: CAUGHT_TIME_MASK (2 bits), CAUGHT_LEVEL_MASK (6 bits), CAUGHT_GENDER_MASK (1 bit), CAUGHT_LOCATION_MASK (7 bits)

source_data.memory_map.md:
## WRAM Bank 0 ($C000-$CFFF)
- wBattleMon: Player active Pokemon (battle_struct)
- wEnemyMon: Enemy active Pokemon
- wPlayerSubStatus1..5, wEnemySubStatus1..5: volatile substatus bytes
- wPlayerStatLevels/wEnemyStatLevels: 7 stages (Atk,Def,Spd,SAtk,SDef,Acc,Eva)
- wPlayerScreens/wEnemyScreens: Spikes, Safeguard, Light Screen, Reflect
- wBattleWeather: Current weather (0-6); wWeatherCount: Turns remaining
- wPlayerDamageTaken, wEnemyDamageTaken: 2 bytes each
- wPlayerStats/wEnemyStats: 5 stats x 2 bytes each
- Turn counters: Rollout, Confuse, Toxic, Disable, Encore, Perish, FuryCutter, Protect

## WRAM Bank 1 ($D000-$DFFF)
- wPlayerName: 8 bytes; wPlayerID: 2 bytes; wMoney: 3 bytes BCD
- wJohtoBadges, wKantoBadges: 1 byte each (8 bits = 8 badges)
- wPartyCount: 0-6; wPartySpecies: 6 bytes + terminator
- wPartyMon1..6: PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48 bytes each
  - Species, Item, Moves (4), Move PP (4), Happiness, DVs (2 bytes)
  - StatExp (5 x 2 bytes), Level, Status, HP (current/max), Stats
  - Caught data (time/level, gender/location)
- wPokedexOwned/Seen: 32 bytes each (256 bits)
- wEventFlags: Large bit array (~1400 flags)

## HRAM ($FF80-$FFFE)
- hRandomAdd, hRandomSub: RNG state
- hMultiplicand (3 bytes), hMultiplier (1 byte), hProduct (4 bytes)
- hDivisor (1 byte), hQuotient (4 bytes)
- hBattleTurn: 0=player, 1=enemy
- hROMBank, hVBlank, hJoyPressed, hJoyDown, hInMenu

## SRAM
- sOptions: text speed, battle style, sound, frame
- sPlayerData, sPartyMon, sPokemonBoxes (14 boxes x 20 Pokemon)
- sMysteryGiftData, sBattleTowerData
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Party/Box Struct Sizes
- PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 48 bytes
- BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 32 bytes
- PARTY_LENGTH = 6 (max party size)
- MONS_PER_BOX = 20 (max Pokemon per box)

### Caught Data Packing
- CAUGHT_TIME_MASK = %11000000 (2 bits for time of day)
- CAUGHT_LEVEL_MASK = %00111111 (6 bits for level, max 63)
- CAUGHT_GENDER_MASK = %10000000 (1 bit for OT gender)
- CAUGHT_LOCATION_MASK = %01111111 (7 bits for location, max 127)

### Sub-Status Flags (5 bytes per side)
SubStatus1: NIGHTMARE, CURSE, PROTECT, IDENTIFIED, PERISH, ENDURE, ROLLOUT, IN_LOVE
SubStatus2: CURLED
SubStatus3: BIDE, RAMPAGE, IN_LOOP, FLINCHED, CHARGED, UNDERGROUND, FLYING, CONFUSED
SubStatus4: X_ACCURACY, MIST, FOCUS_ENERGY, SUBSTITUTE, RECHARGE, RAGE, LEECH_SEED
SubStatus5: TOXIC, TRANSFORMED, ENCORED, LOCK_ON, DESTINY_BOND, CANT_RUN

### Screen Flags
SCREENS_SPIKES=bit0, SCREENS_SAFEGUARD=bit2, SCREENS_LIGHT_SCREEN=bit3, SCREENS_REFLECT=bit4

### Weather Constants
WEATHER_NONE=0, WEATHER_RAIN=1, WEATHER_SUN=2, WEATHER_SANDSTORM=3
WEATHER_RAIN_END=4, WEATHER_SUN_END=5, WEATHER_SANDSTORM_END=6

### Move Struct (7 bytes)
Offset 0: MOVE_ANIM, 1: MOVE_EFFECT, 2: MOVE_POWER, 3: MOVE_TYPE, 4: MOVE_ACC, 5: MOVE_PP, 6: MOVE_CHANCE

unstructured_notes: The battle union at $C600+ is key: many unrelated data structures share the same WRAM region depending on game mode. In Rust, model this as an enum or tagged union. The PARTYMON struct differs from BOXMON by 16 bytes — party includes current HP, stats (10 bytes), and PP Up bits. Box Pokemon only store DVs, StatExp, moves, PP, and basic metadata, not computed stats. The caught_data byte pair packs two separate concerns: byte 1 has CAUGHT_TIME (2 bits) + CAUGHT_LEVEL (6 bits); byte 2 has CAUGHT_GENDER (1 bit) + CAUGHT_LOCATION (7 bits). This sprint depends on Sprint 2 (constants) and is prerequisite for Sprints 16-17 (stat calc) and Sprint 19 (RNG, uses HRAM fields). The wEventFlags bit array maps directly to the event_flags.asm file with ~1400 named bits — implement as a Vec<u64> with named accessor methods, not a huge struct. SRAM integrity in Crystal uses a checksum; simulate it for save/load correctness.

goal: QA audit of sprints 13-14 — verify item data, pocket assignments, prices, memory layout against pokecrystal
abstract_tasks: Cross-check all item prices against all_items.md tables: verify FULL_RESTORE=3000, MAX_POTION=2500, HYPER_POTION=1200, SUPER_POTION=700, POTION=300, all X items, evolution stones, Apricorn balls at 150
Verify all 4 bag pockets are correctly defined: Items (medicine/vitamins/battle items/held), Balls (Poke Ball through Park Ball), Key Items (bicycle through GS Ball), TMs/HMs (TM01-TM50 + HM01-HM07)
Validate PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=48 and BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH=32 are correctly enforced; check that all 48 fields are present in party struct and box struct omits computed stats
Test memory layout for all 5 SubStatus byte definitions: confirm bit positions match game_constants.md exactly (e.g. SUBSTATUS_NIGHTMARE=bit0 of SubStatus1, SUBSTATUS_TOXIC=bit0 of SubStatus5)
Verify caught data packing round-trips: pack and unpack time/level/gender/location bits and confirm no data loss for edge values (level 63, location 127, time 3, gender 1)
Check wEventFlags bit array: verify at least 10 key story flags are addressable by name and return correct default (0) on a fresh save
Validate SRAM structure ordering matches pokecrystal sram.asm: sOptions first, then player data, then party, then PC boxes

source_data.memory_map.md:
## Key RAM Relationships for Battle Simulation
1. wBattleMon/wEnemyMon: Active Pokemon stats
2. wPlayerSubStatus1-5/wEnemySubStatus1-5: All volatile effects
3. wPlayerStatLevels/wEnemyStatLevels: Stat stages (-6 to +6, stored 1-13)
4. wPlayerScreens/wEnemyScreens: Field effects
5. wBattleWeather/wWeatherCount: Weather
6. wPlayerDamageTaken/wEnemyDamageTaken: For Counter/Mirror Coat
7. wCurPlayerMove/wCurEnemyMove: Selected moves
8. Turn counters: Confusion, toxic, disable, encore, perish, wrap
source_data.game_constants.md:
PARTYMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 48, BOXMON_STRUCT_LENGTH = 32, PARTY_LENGTH = 6, MONS_PER_BOX = 20
Num stats tracked in StatLevels: 7 (Atk,Def,Spd,SAtk,SDef,Acc,Eva) + 1 unused
BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (neutral, maps to stage 0), MAX_STAT_LEVEL = 13 (maps to stage +6)

unstructured_notes: Key gotcha: item prices must be verified against pokecrystal data/items/ ASM files, not just the compiled reference. Type-boost item Dragon Scale vs Dragon Fang bug is a deliberate recreation target — Dragon Scale should be the booster in the Rust implementation. MysteryBerry restores 5 PP (fixed, not all PP). PP Up increases max PP — the 2-bit PP Up field per move is part of the party struct, not item_details. Guard Spec is a battle item applied to the FIELD (prevents stat lowering for the whole party for 5 turns), not just the user. Sacred Ash quantity is always 1 (key item category in some analyses but is a regular bag item that can be bought from Battle Tower). Verify that wEventFlags returns 0 for fresh game state and that no flags are pre-set in the initial memory layout.

goal: Implement DV system — 4 stored DVs, derived HP DV, shared Special DV, gender determination from Attack DV, shiny check
abstract_tasks: Implement DV storage: two bytes encoding Attack(high nibble)+Defense(low nibble) in byte 1, Speed(high nibble)+Special(low nibble) in byte 2, all values 0-15
Implement HP DV derivation formula: HP_DV = (Attack_DV & 1)*8 + (Defense_DV & 1)*4 + (Speed_DV & 1)*2 + (Special_DV & 1)
Implement Special DV sharing: both SpAtk and SpDef use the same single Special DV value from byte 2 low nibble
Implement gender determination from Attack DV: compare against species gender ratio threshold (0=always male, 255=genderless, 2/4/8/12/14 for various ratios); if Attack_DV >= threshold -> male
Implement shiny check: shiny if Defense_DV==10 AND Speed_DV==10 AND Special_DV==10 AND Attack_DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
Implement DV generation for wild Pokemon: call RNG twice, first byte = Attack DV (high nibble) + Defense DV (low nibble), second byte = Speed DV (high nibble) + Special DV (low nibble)
Write unit tests validating HP DV derivation for edge cases (all DVs 0, all DVs 15, mixed nibbles) and shiny probability (~1/8192)

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Determinant Values (DVs)
DVs are Gen 2's equivalent of IVs. Range: 0-15 for each stat.

### DV Storage
- Byte 1: AAAA DDDD (high nibble = Attack DV, low nibble = Defense DV)
- Byte 2: SSSS PPPP (high nibble = Speed DV, low nibble = Special DV)

### HP DV (derived)
HP_DV = (Attack_DV & 1)*8 + (Defense_DV & 1)*4 + (Speed_DV & 1)*2 + (Special_DV & 1)
Range: 0-15, determined by other 4 DVs.

### Special DV
Shared between Special Attack and Special Defense. Both use same value.

## Gender Determination
Gender determined by Attack DV vs species gender threshold.
If Attack_DV >= threshold: Male. If < threshold: Female.
Thresholds: 0=always male, 2=87.5% male, 4=75%, 8=50%, 12=25%, 14=12.5%, 255=genderless
Consequence: Female Pokemon never have Attack DV >= gender threshold.
For 87.5% male species (starter, Eevee, threshold=2), females can only have Atk DV 0 or 1.

## Shininess
Shiny if: Defense DV=10, Speed DV=10, Special DV=10, Attack DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
Probability: ~1/8192 (5 valid Attack DVs x (1/16)^3 for the other three)
HP DV for shinies is always 0 or 8 (since Def/Spd/Spc are all even=10)
source_data.game_constants.md:
### Shiny DVs
- ATKDEFDV_SHINY = $EA (Atk=14, Def=10)
- SPDSPCDV_SHINY = $AA (Spd=10, Spc=10)
- Shiny requires: Atk DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}, others = 10
BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (stored stage for neutral; actual stage = stored - 7)
Stat stages stored as 1-13, neutral is 7

unstructured_notes: The 'Special DV' being shared between SpAtk and SpDef is a fundamental Gen 2 quirk — when you calculate Special Attack, use the Special DV; when you calculate Special Defense, use the SAME Special DV. This is why high-Special-DV Pokemon are valuable for both offensive and defensive special stats. Female Pokemon of 87.5% male ratio species (starters, Eevee) are locked to Attack DV 0 or 1, capping their Attack stat severely — this matters for competitive play. Shiny DVs: the ATKDEFDV_SHINY constant $EA decodes as Atk=14 (high nibble of $E=14), Def=10 (low nibble of $A=10). Valid shiny Attack DVs {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15} are the 8 values where the low bit of Atk could be 0 or 1 — wait, actually: 2=0010(bit0=0), 3=0011(bit0=1), 6=0110(bit0=0), 7=0111(bit0=1), 10=1010(bit0=0), 11=1011(bit0=1), 14=1110(bit0=0), 15=1111(bit0=1). These are all values where bits 1 and 3 are set OR bits 1 and 3 are clear with bit0 varying... actually these are numbers >= 2 where the top bit pattern produces Def DV parity. Depends on Sprint 14 (memory layout for DV byte storage in PartyMon struct).

goal: Implement stat calculation — HP formula, other stat formula, stat experience contribution, level-up recalculation
abstract_tasks: Implement HP formula: floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + Level + 10, using integer arithmetic with correct rounding
Implement other stats formula: floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + 5, for Attack/Defense/Speed/SpAtk/SpDef
Implement StatExp contribution: floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) / 4) — note use of ceil(sqrt()) not floor(sqrt()); max contribution is 64 at StatExp=65535
Implement stat recalculation triggers: on level-up, on evolution, on PC deposit/withdraw, on vitamin use; NOT continuously during battle
Implement vitamin stat experience gain: each vitamin adds 2560 to the stat's StatExp, capped at 25600 (cannot raise above 25600 with vitamins)
Verify example: Level 50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp=65535): inner=(154+15)*2+64=402, stat=floor(402*50/100)+5=201+5=206
Write tests for: minimum stats (base=1, DV=0, StatExp=0, Lv1), maximum stats (base=255, DV=15, StatExp=65535, Lv100), and the specific Mewtwo example

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Stat Formulas
HP = floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + Level + 10
Other = floor(((Base + DV)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(StatExp))/4)) * Level / 100) + 5

Max StatExp contribution: floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = floor(256/4) = 64

## Stat Experience
Gain: when defeating enemy, each participating mon gains StatExp equal to defeated species base stats.
Accumulation: HP+=base HP, Atk+=base Atk, Def+=base Def, Spd+=base Spd, Spc+=base Spc (shared)
No cap on total (all 5 stats can be maxed at 65535)
Vitamins add 2560, capped at 25600 per stat.

## Stat Recalculation
Recalculated: on level-up, on evolution, on PC deposit/withdraw, on vitamin use
NOT continuously in battle (battle uses stat stages only)

## Example: Lv50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp)
Inner = (154+15)*2 + 64 = 338 + 64 = 402
Stat = floor(402*50/100) + 5 = 201 + 5 = 206
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Stat Calculation
- Base: Species base stat (0-255)
- DV: Determinant Value (0-15)
- StatExp: Stat Experience (0-65535)
- Level: 1-100
Source: engine/pokemon/mon_stats.asm — CalcMonStats

## Paralysis and Burn Stat Effects
Paralysis: Speed * 25% (quartered), applied to battle stat directly
Burn: Attack * 50% (halved), affects physical damage only
Critical hit + burn: if attacker Atk stage >= defender Def stage, burn halving NOT ignored

unstructured_notes: The key implementation trap is ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) vs floor(sqrt(StatExp)). The formula uses ceil, meaning sqrt(65535)=255.998..., ceil=256, /4=64. Using floor would give 255/4=63. Always use ceiling for the sqrt step. StatExp is per-stat, not shared — the Special StatExp is used for BOTH SpAtk and SpDef calculations. Paralysis and Burn effects on stats are battle-only multipliers applied AFTER CalcMonStats produces the base stat — they do not modify the party data stat values permanently. At Lv1, HP formula: ((base+DV)*2+0)*1/100+1+10 which floors to 0 for most species, producing HP=11. A Shedinja-like case (base HP=1) at Lv1 with DV=0 StatExp=0: floor((1+0)*2/100)+1+10=0+11=11 still. Minimum meaningful stat values need testing. Depends on Sprint 16 (DV system).

goal: QA audit of sprints 16-17 — verify stat formulas with known examples (Lv50 Mewtwo), DV edge cases, gender thresholds
abstract_tasks: Verify the Mewtwo SpAtk example: Lv50, base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp=65535 should produce 206; run the calculation through the implementation and assert exact match
Test HP formula at boundary: Lv1 Pokemon with base_hp=45 (Bulbasaur), DV=0, StatExp=0 should give floor((45+0)*2*1/100)+1+10=11; Lv100 max: floor((45+15)*2+64)*1)+100+10=240
Verify DV edge cases: all-0 DVs produce the minimum stat for a given species/level; all-15 DVs produce the maximum; DV=8 produces exactly midpoint
Test gender thresholds for all ratio categories: species with threshold=2 and Attack DV=2 must be male, Attack DV=1 must be female; threshold=255 is always genderless regardless of Attack DV
Test shiny detection: DVs (Atk=14, Def=10, Spd=10, Spc=10) must return shiny=true; DVs (Atk=15, Def=10, Spd=10, Spc=10) must return shiny=false; verify HP DV for shiny is 0 or 8
Verify StatExp capping: vitamins cannot raise StatExp above 25600; defeating Pokemon raises StatExp without cap (up to 65535); adding StatExp past 65535 saturates at 65535
Test Special DV sharing: SpAtk and SpDef calculation for same Pokemon use identical DV value, not separate

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Example Calculations
Lv50 Mewtwo SpAtk (base=154, DV=15, maxStatExp=65535):
Inner = (154+15)*2 + floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = 338 + 64 = 402
Stat = floor(402*50/100) + 5 = 201 + 5 = 206

Max StatExp contribution = floor(ceil(sqrt(65535))/4) = floor(256/4) = 64

## Shiny Conditions
Defense DV=10, Speed DV=10, Special DV=10, Attack DV in {2,3,6,7,10,11,14,15}
HP DV for shinies: (Atk&1)*8 + 0 + 0 + 0 = 0 or 8
Probability: 8/16 * 1/16 * 1/16 * 1/16 = 8/65536 = 1/8192

## Gender Thresholds
0: Always male (all DVs male)
2: 87.5% male (threshold means Attack DV 0,1 = female; 2-15 = male)
4: 75% male (Attack DV 0-3 = female; 4-15 = male)
8: 50% male (0-7 female; 8-15 male)
12: 25% male (0-11 female; 12-15 male)
14: 12.5% male (0-13 female; 14-15 male)
255: Genderless (never satisfied >= 255)
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Stat Stages
| Stage | Multiplier | Fraction |
Stages -6 to +6, stored as 1-13 (BASE_STAT_LEVEL=7 is neutral)
Accuracy/Evasion use different fractions (3/9 through 9/3)

## Paralysis Speed Reduction
Paralyzed Pokemon: Speed * 25% (quartered)
Burn Attack Reduction: Attack * 50% (halved, physical moves only)
Critical hit crit-stat check: if attacker Atk stage >= defender Def stage, burn ignored
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 14: Focus Energy + Critical Hit
Gen 2 Focus Energy correctly adds +1 to crit stage (unlike Gen 1 bug).
With Focus Energy + Slash (high-crit): stage 3 = 85/256 (~33%) crit rate.
## Scenario 12: Sleep Counter on Switch
Sleep counter preserved in party data when switching out. Stored in low 3 bits of MON_STATUS.

unstructured_notes: Critical testing note: the formula uses ceil(sqrt(StatExp)) not floor. At StatExp=65535, sqrt=255.998, ceil=256, /4=64. At StatExp=65536 (impossible, max is 65535), same result. At StatExp=0, contribution=0. At StatExp=1, sqrt=1, ceil=1, /4=0 (rounds down). At StatExp=15, sqrt=3.87, ceil=4, /4=1. These edge points are important for boundary tests. Gender determination has a real gameplay consequence: since female starters can only have Attack DV 0 or 1, their max Attack stat is significantly lower — this is authentic Crystal behavior. The QA agent should run simulation tests comparing against known Pokemon Crystal save file stat values where available.

goal: Implement RNG system — hardware RNG equivalent (PRNG for recreation), battle PRNG, link-synchronized PRNG
abstract_tasks: Implement hardware RNG substitute: use a fast PRNG (xorshift or similar) seeded at battle/game start instead of the DIV-register-based hardware RNG, returning a single random byte
Implement battle PRNG (BattleRandom): for non-link battles, use the hardware RNG substitute; for link battles, use the LCG stream system
Implement link battle LCG: 10-byte seed array (wLinkBattleRNs), counter (wLinkBattleRNCount); advance formula: new_seed = (old_seed * 5 + 1) & 0xFF; consume 9 values then regenerate all 10
Implement RandomRange(n): unbiased random int in [0, n) using rejection sampling — compute bias=256%n, reject if result < (256-bias), then take result%n
Implement per-frame VBlank RNG advancement for simulation accuracy: even during menu/idle states, advance RNG state every simulated frame
Expose an interface allowing the simulation to either use deterministic seeded PRNG (for testing) or non-deterministic PRNG (for gameplay)
Write distribution tests: call BattleRandom 256000 times, verify each byte value appears approximately 1000 times (uniform distribution within 5%)

source_data.rng_mechanics.md:
## Two RNG Systems
1. Hardware RNG (Random): reads hardware DIV register (16384 Hz); advances in VBlank too; returns hRandomSub
   NOT deterministic from seed. For recreation: use seeded PRNG instead.
2. Battle PRNG (BattleRandom): non-link = calls Random; link = uses LCG stream:
   - 10 seeds in wLinkBattleRNs, position in wLinkBattleRNCount
   - Advance: new = (old * 5 + 1) & 0xFF (LCG: a=5, c=1, m=256)
   - Consume 9 values per generation (off-by-one: cp SERIAL_RNS_LENGTH-1 = cp 9, index 8 triggers regen)
   - 10th value from old set returned while new set generated
   - Period: 256 per seed

## RandomRange
Unbiased: b = 256 % c; call Random until result < (256-b); return result % c

## VBlank RNG Advancement
Every frame (~59.7Hz): adds DIV to hRandomAdd, subtracts DIV from hRandomSub
Means RNG changes every frame even when idle — constant entropy injection

## Implementation Notes
1. Do NOT use hardware RNG. Use seeded PRNG.
2. Link battle PRNG is the only deterministic option.
3. BattleRandom vs Random distinction matters for replay determinism.
4. RNG consumption ORDER is critical — crit before accuracy = different outcomes.
5. Link battle LCG subtle off-by-one: index 8 (not 9) triggers regeneration.
source_data.core_routines.md:
## Random (home/random.asm)
Hardware-based. Returns hRandomSub in a. Called from VBlank.
NOT suitable for link battles (would desync).

## BattleRandom
Deterministic PRNG for battle. Uses 10-byte stream (wLinkBattleRNCount tracks position).
Shared seed ensures link battle sync. Returns random byte in a.

## RandomRange
Returns random in [0, a). Uses rejection sampling to avoid modulo bias.

unstructured_notes: The off-by-one in the link battle LCG is important: the PRNG stream has 10 slots but only 9 are consumed before regeneration. Index 8 (the 9th slot, 0-indexed) triggers regeneration; the 10th slot (index 9) from the OLD set is returned as the result while the NEW set is being computed. This subtlety means a fresh stream effectively gives you 9 values + 1 old value before full refresh. For the recreation: in non-link battles, use xorshift128 or a similar fast PRNG seeded with a u64. In link battles, implement the LCG exactly. The consumption ORDER of RNG calls in the battle engine is critical — damage variation is called AFTER accuracy, which is called AFTER critical hit check, etc. Getting this order wrong will produce correct individual probabilities but wrong correlated outcomes. This sprint is a prerequisite for sprints 22-23 (damage formula) since damage variation uses BattleRandom.

goal: Implement stat stages — 7 stats, levels 1-13, multiplier table (25/100 to 400/100), stage application to raw stats
abstract_tasks: Implement stat stage storage: 7 stats per side (Atk, Def, Spd, SpAtk, SpDef, Acc, Eva), stored as u8 values 1-13 where 7=neutral (stage 0), 1=stage -6, 13=stage +6
Implement attack/defense/speed multiplier table: stages 1-13 map to fractions 2/8, 2/7, 2/6, 2/5, 2/4, 2/3, 2/2, 3/2, 4/2, 5/2, 6/2, 7/2, 8/2 (i.e., 25% through 400%)
Implement accuracy/evasion multiplier table: stages 1-13 map to fractions 3/9, 3/8, 3/7, 3/6, 3/5, 3/4, 3/3, 4/3, 5/3, 6/3, 7/3, 8/3, 9/3
Implement stat stage application: apply stage multiplier to the base computed stat value, truncating to integer; enforce minimum stat value of 1
Implement paralysis speed reduction: multiply Speed by 25% (divide by 4) AFTER stat stage is applied, truncating to integer
Implement burn attack reduction: multiply Attack by 50% (divide by 2) AFTER stat stage is applied for physical moves only
Implement stage change bounds: stages cannot go below 1 or above 13; stage change fails if already at the limit with appropriate message

source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Stat Stages (In-Battle Modifiers)
Range: -6 to +6 (stored 1-13, BASE_STAT_LEVEL=7=neutral)

### Attack/Defense/SpAtk/SpDef/Speed Multipliers
Stage -6: 2/8 (0.25)
Stage -5: 2/7 (0.286)
Stage -4: 2/6 (0.333)
Stage -3: 2/5 (0.40)
Stage -2: 2/4 (0.50)
Stage -1: 2/3 (0.667)
Stage  0: 2/2 (1.00)
Stage +1: 3/2 (1.50)
Stage +2: 4/2 (2.00)
Stage +3: 5/2 (2.50)
Stage +4: 6/2 (3.00)
Stage +5: 7/2 (3.50)
Stage +6: 8/2 (4.00)

### Accuracy/Evasion Multipliers
Stage -6: 3/9; -5: 3/8; -4: 3/7; -3: 3/6; -2: 3/5; -1: 3/4
Stage  0: 3/3; +1: 4/3; +2: 5/3; +3: 6/3; +4: 7/3; +5: 8/3; +6: 9/3

Accuracy formula: EffectiveAccuracy = MoveAccuracy * AccStageMultiplier * EvaStageMultiplier
If Foresight active AND target evasion stage > attacker accuracy stage: bypass evasion/accuracy entirely.

## Status Effects on Stats
Paralysis: Speed * 25% (quartered), applied after stat stage
Burn: Attack * 50% (halved), applied after stat stage for physical moves
Critical hit: if attacker Atk stage >= target Def stage, burn halving ignored (and all stages ignored)
source_data.game_constants.md:
BASE_STAT_LEVEL = 7 (default/neutral stat stage, stored value)
MAX_STAT_LEVEL = 13 (maximum stored value, = stage +6)
Stat stages: 7 stats tracked (Atk,Def,Spd,SAtk,SDef,Acc,Eva) + 1 unused byte
wPlayerStatLevels, wEnemyStatLevels: 8 bytes each (7 stats + padding)
source_data.move_effects_complete.md: Stat stage limits: stages cannot go above MAX_STAT_LEVEL or below 1. CheckFail condition for already-maxed/minimized stat. Haze resets ALL stat stages to BASE_STAT_LEVEL (7) for both sides. Psych Up copies all 7 stat stages from target to user. Baton Pass: all stat stages transferred to incoming Pokemon.

unstructured_notes: The stored value is 1-13, not -6 to +6. To convert: stage = stored_value - 7. A stored value of 7 = stage 0. All stat stage change moves modify the stored byte. When implementing Haze, reset all 14 stat level bytes (7 per side) to 7 (BASE_STAT_LEVEL). When implementing Psych Up, copy the opponent's 7 stat level bytes over the user's 7 stat level bytes. The accuracy check formula is: generate a random byte, compare to EffectiveAccuracy = (MoveBaseAccuracy * AccMultiplier_num / AccMultiplier_den) * (EvaMultiplier_den / EvaMultiplier_num) truncated to 0-255. The evasion stage effectively inverts: stage +6 evasion makes moves less likely to hit. Foresight/Mind Reader interaction: if SUBSTATUS_IDENTIFIED is set on the target AND target's evasion stage > attacker's accuracy stage, skip the evasion/accuracy modifier entirely (always use raw move accuracy). Depends on Sprints 16-17 (stat system) and Sprint 19 (RNG for battle calculations).

goal: QA audit of sprints 19-20 — verify RNG distribution, stat stage multipliers, edge cases at min/max stages
abstract_tasks: Run 1,000,000 BattleRandom calls and verify uniform byte distribution: each value 0-255 should appear ~3906 times; check chi-squared test passes at p=0.01
Verify stat multiplier at each of the 13 stages: apply stage to a base stat of 100 and confirm results: stages give 25,29,33,40,50,67,100,150,200,250,300,350,400 (rounded to integer)
Test stat stage clamping: attempt to raise an already-maxed (+6, stored=13) stat stage and verify it returns a failure code and leaves the stage at 13
Test stat stage clamping: attempt to lower an already-minimized (-6, stored=1) stat stage and verify failure and stored value remains 1
Verify paralysis speed reduction: a Pokemon with 100 Speed and paralysis should compute effective speed as 25; at stage +6 paralyzed, should be floor(400*25/100)=100
Verify burn attack halving: a Pokemon with 100 Attack and burn, stage +2, should compute effective attack as floor(200*50/100)=100
Test Foresight evasion bypass: target with +6 evasion, attacker with 0 accuracy stage, Foresight active — move should hit at its base accuracy percentage (not reduced by evasion)

source_data.rng_mechanics.md:
## RNG Consumption Order (critical for determinism)
Battle RNG consumers:
Accuracy check, Critical hit check, Damage variation, Move effect chance,
Confusion self-hit (50%), Infatuation (50%), Paralysis (25%),
Sleep counter (1-7 turns), Disable duration, Encore duration, Confusion duration,
Multi-hit count, Metronome, Present power, Magnitude, AI decisions,
Quick Claw activation, Speed tie, Protect/Detect consecutive check, Sleep Talk selection

## LCG Properties
Formula: next = (current * 5 + 1) & 0xFF
Period: 256. Full-period LCG (odd multiplier, odd c, power-of-2 m).
9 of 10 stream values consumed per generation cycle (index 8 triggers regen).
source_data.stat_calculation.md:
## Stat Stage Table (derived multipliers from fraction tables)
Atk/Def/Spd stages (-6 to +6): 25, 29, 33, 40, 50, 67, 100, 150, 200, 250, 300, 350, 400 (percent)
Acc/Eva stages: 33, 38, 43, 50, 60, 75, 100, 133, 167, 200, 233, 267, 300 (percent)
Paralysis: Speed * 25%
Burn: Attack * 50% (physical only)
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Stat Stages
Stored as 1-13. BASE_STAT_LEVEL=7 (neutral). Accuracy/Evasion use different table.
Source: data/battle/stat_multipliers.asm, data/battle/accuracy_multipliers.asm

## Critical Hit Stages
Stage 0: 17/256 (~6.6%)
Stage 1: 32/256 (~12.5%)
Stage 2: 64/256 (25%)
Stage 3: 85/256 (~33%)
Stage 4+: 128/256 (50%)
High crit moves: +2 to stage. Focus Energy/Scope Lens: +1. Max effective stage: 4.
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 20: 1/256 Effect Miss Glitch
Even 100% accuracy moves have 1/256 miss chance. Random byte 255 compared with cp means 255 is not < 255.
Source: BattleCommand_CheckHit generates random byte, compares cp b where b=255.

## Scenario 13: Protect Success Rates
First use: always. Second consecutive: 1/3. Third: 1/7. Fourth: 1/15.
Pattern: threshold = 255 >> ProtectCount (right-shift). wProtectCount tracks consecutive uses.

unstructured_notes: When testing the stat multipliers, note that they use integer division: floor(stat * numerator / denominator). For base stat 100 at stage -1: floor(100 * 2 / 3) = floor(200/3) = 66, not 67. The table says 67% but the actual result for 100 base is 66 due to truncation. Confirm the implementation matches the integer truncation behavior, not rounding. The 1/256 miss bug must be preserved: implement the accuracy check as 'if random_byte < effective_accuracy then hit else miss' where effective_accuracy is clamped to 255 max. A 100% accuracy move has effective_accuracy=255; random_byte=255 fails the check (255 < 255 is false). The Protect consecutive success check uses right-shift: threshold = 255 >> n where n=0 means always hit (255 < 255 fails, so wait — actually the first use SUCCEEDS because ProtectCount=0, and they use a different comparison for first use).

goal: Implement damage formula — phase 1 (base damage), phase 2 (physical/special split, screens), phase 5 (TruncateHL_BC), phase 6 (self-destruct)
abstract_tasks: Implement Phase 1 base damage: ((2*Level/5 + 2) * Power * Attack / Defense) / 50, using strict integer truncation at each division step in this exact order: 2*Level then /5 then +2 then *Power then *Attack then /Defense then /50

Implement Phase 2 physical/special split: physical moves (types Normal through Rock/Steel) use Attack vs Defense; special moves (types Fire through Dark) use SpAtk vs SpDef; screens double the defensive stat before stage application
Implement Reflect: doubles the target's Defense value (16-bit left shift) before dividing in Phase 1; this can overflow past 1024 in link battles
Implement Light Screen: doubles the target's SpDef value before dividing in Phase 1; same overflow behavior as Reflect

Implement TruncateHL_BC (Phase 5): iteratively right-shift both Attack and Defense by 2 bits each until both fit in 8 bits; in single-player runs twice (additional overflow check); in link battles runs once only
Implement Phase 6 self-destruct/explosion: halve the target's Defense value (right-shift by 1, minimum 1) when move has EFFECT_SELFDESTRUCT, applied BEFORE the damage formula
Add base damage minimum: result of Phase 1 is capped at minimum of 2 (any attacking move that hits deals at least 2 damage before modifiers)

source_data.damage_formula.md:
## Phase 1: Base Damage (BattleCommand_DamageCalc)
BaseDamage = ((2*Level/5 + 2) * Power * Attack / Defense) / 50
Computation order (each division truncates):
1. 2 * Level (overflow: if > 255, high byte set to 1)
2. /5
3. +2
4. * Power
5. * Attack
6. / Defense (minimum Defense = 1)
7. / 50

## Phase 2: Stat Selection and Screens
Physical moves (type < SPECIAL): Attack vs Defense. Reflect doubles Defense.
Special moves (type >= SPECIAL): SpAtk vs SpDef. Light Screen doubles SpDef.
Screens: sla c; rl b in ASM — can overflow past 1024 causing Glitches.

## Phase 5: TruncateHL_BC
16-bit Attack and Defense iteratively right-shifted by 2 until both fit in 8 bits.
Single-player: runs twice (checks h or b again). Link battles: runs once only.
Values >1024 before truncation can wrap around (overflow bug preserved).

## Phase 6: Self-Destruct/Explosion
If EFFECT_SELFDESTRUCT: target Defense halved (srl c). Minimum Defense = 1.
Defense halving occurs BEFORE damage formula. User HP set to 0 BEFORE damage.

## Phase 9: Damage Cap
Raw damage capped at 997, then +2 added: final max = 999, minimum = 2.
source_data.formulas_reference.md:
## Damage Formula
Phases (exact order):
1. Base: ((2*Level/5+2)*Power*A/D)/50 + 2
2. Critical hit: 2x (bypasses stat stages for relevant stat comparison)
3. Weather: Rain+Water 1.5x, Rain+Fire 0.5x, Sun+Fire 1.5x, Sun+Water 0.5x
4. Badge boost: 1.125x player only
5. STAB: 1.5x
6. Type effectiveness: 2x/0.5x/0x per type
7. Damage variation: random 217-255, /255 (85-100%)
8. Item boosts: Thick Club 2x Atk (Marowak), Light Ball 2x SpAtk (Pikachu), Metal Powder 1.5x Def (Ditto)
Cap: capped at 997 before variation, final max 997 (note: damage_formula.md says 999 = 997+2).

unstructured_notes: There is a discrepancy between formulas_reference.md (cap at 997) and damage_formula.md (cap 997 then +2=999). The ASM source (damage_formula.md) is more specific: the cap is 997 then the '+2' from Phase 1 formula is added separately, making the effective max 999. Use damage_formula.md as the authoritative source. The physical/special split uses a comparison against a SPECIAL constant (a type ID value): types with ID < SPECIAL are physical, >= SPECIAL are special. In Crystal, the SPECIAL boundary falls after Ghost/Rock/Steel (physical) and before Fire/Water/Grass/Electric (special). The TruncateHL_BC overflow behavior is a BUG to preserve: when Attack or Defense exceeds 1024 (10 bits), the iterative right-shift wraps and produces wrong values. Single-player runs the truncation check twice, link battles once — this is a specific divergence between modes that must be replicated. Self-destruct sets user HP=0 BEFORE dealing damage — user is dead even if the move misses (e.g., against a Ghost type). Depends on Sprints 16-17 (stats), 19 (RNG), 20 (stat stages).

goal: Implement damage modifiers — critical hits, weather, badge boosts, STAB, type effectiveness (dual-type), damage variation (85-100%), item boosts, Thick Club/Light Ball/Metal Powder
abstract_tasks: Implement critical hit check: generate random byte, compare to threshold from crit stage table (stage 0=17, 1=32, 2=64, 3=85, 4+=128 out of 256); on crit apply 2x multiplier (cap at 0xFFFF) and conditionally bypass stat stages per Phase 3 logic

Implement critical hit stat bypass (Phase 3): on crit, if target defense stage >= attacker offense stage, ignore ALL stat stage modifiers, burn halving, and Reflect/Light Screen; if attacker stage > target stage, keep boosts normally
Implement STAB: if move type matches either of the user's types, Damage = Damage + Damage/2 (1.5x, integer truncation)

Implement type effectiveness: look up attacking_type vs each defending_type in type chart; multiply by 20/divide by 10 for super effective, multiply by 5/divide by 10 for not very effective, multiply by 0 for immune (also sets AttackMissed); dual-typed defenders have both types checked sequentially
Implement weather modifiers: Rain+Water 1.5x, Rain+Fire 0.5x, Sun+Fire 1.5x, Sun+Water 0.5x, Rain+SolarBeam 0.5x; minimum 1 damage after weather
Implement badge type boosts: for player's Pokemon only (not link/BT), add Damage/8 per matching badge type (1.125x); minimum boost is 1 if Damage/8 rounds to 0
Implement damage variation: generate random byte; right-rotate; if result >= 217 use it else regenerate; multiply Damage by result, divide by 255; effective range 85-100%

Implement item boosts: Thick Club doubles Attack if held by Cubone/Marowak (physical), Light Ball doubles SpAtk if held by Pikachu (special), Metal Powder multiplies Defense by 1.5 if held by Ditto; all can cause 16-bit overflow (preserve bug)

source_data.damage_formula.md:
## Phase 8: Critical Hit Multiplier
Crit: Damage * 2 (left shift). Capped at $FFFF (65535).

## Phase 3: Critical Hit Stat Handling
On crit: if target defensive stage >= attacker offensive stage: ignore ALL stat stages, burn halving, Reflect/Light Screen.
If attacker offensive stage > target defensive stage: keep stages normally.

## Phase 10: STAB
Damage = Damage + Damage/2 (1.5x, truncated)

## Phase 13: Type Effectiveness
Per defending type: super effective = *20/10 (2x), not very = *5/10 (0.5x), immune = *0/10
Dual-typed: both applied sequentially. Minimum 1 damage if non-zero effectiveness.
Possible net multipliers: 0x, 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, 4x.

## Phase 11: Weather Modifiers
Rain + Water: *15/10 (1.5x); Rain + Fire: *5/10 (0.5x)
Sun + Fire: *15/10 (1.5x); Sun + Water: *5/10 (0.5x)
Rain + SolarBeam: *5/10 (0.5x). Minimum 1 damage.

## Phase 12: Badge Boosts (player only, not link/BT)
Damage = Damage + Damage/8. Minimum boost 1 if Damage/8=0.
Badge-to-type: Zephyr=Flying, Hive=Bug, Plain=Normal, Fog=Ghost, Mineral=Steel,
Storm=Fighting, Glacier=Ice, Rising=Dragon, Boulder=Rock, Cascade=Water,
Thunder=Electric, Rainbow=Grass, Soul=Poison, Marsh=Psychic, Volcano=Fire, Earth=Ground

## Phase 14: Damage Variation
Random byte right-rotated, must be >= 217. Multiply Damage by result, /256.
Effective range: 85-100%.

## Phase 4: Species-Specific Item Boosts
Thick Club: doubles Attack if Cubone/Marowak (physical). Can overflow.
Light Ball: doubles SpAtk if Pikachu (special). Can overflow.
Metal Powder: multiplies Defense by 1.5 if Ditto (applied after TruncateHL_BC).

## Phase 7: Type-Boosting Items
Damage = Damage * (100 + ItemBonus) / 100. ItemBonus typically 10.
BUG: Dragon Scale boosts Dragon (should be Dragon Fang). BUG: affects confusion self-hit.
source_data.type_chart.md:
17x17 type effectiveness matrix (17 types + CURSE type).
Physical types: Normal, Fighting, Flying, Poison, Ground, Rock, Bug, Ghost, Steel
Special types: Fire, Water, Grass, Electric, Psychic, Ice, Dragon, Dark
Key immunities: Normal/Fighting -> Ghost, Electric -> Ground, Ground -> Flying, Poison -> Steel,
Fire/Water/Ice/Electric -> (check chart), Dragon -> (check chart), Ghost -> Psychic
Sandstorm: Rock/Ground/Steel types immune to Sandstorm residual damage.

unstructured_notes: The damage variation uses a specific right-rotate-then-rejection method: generate a random byte (0-255), right-rotate it (bit 0 moves to bit 7), check if result >= 217; if not, try again. This means the range 217-255 is used, producing 85-100% (217/256 = ~84.8%). The type effectiveness chain for dual-typed defenders can produce 0x (immune), 0.25x, 0.5x, 1x, 2x, or 4x total multipliers. If EITHER type is immune (0), the total is 0 and AttackMissed is set. Badge boosts apply per MOVE TYPE (not per physical/special category). The Fog Badge boosts Ghost-type moves, which is only Lick and Shadow Ball in Gen 2 — important detail since many Ghost moves in later gens didn't exist. The 16 badge types map to 16 type constants. Critical hit behavior with stat stages: this is one of the most complex rules in Gen 2 battle. Exactly one comparison is made: if the DEFENSIVE stat stage (stored, 1-13) >= OFFENSIVE stat stage (stored, 1-13), bypass everything. The 'offensive' stat is Attack for physical moves, SpAtk for special moves. Depends on Sprints 19 (RNG), 20 (stat stages), 22 (base damage phase).

goal: QA audit of sprints 22-23 — verify damage calc with 36 battle scenarios, overflow behavior, screen interaction with crits
abstract_tasks: Run all 36 documented battle scenarios from battle_scenarios.md and verify expected outcomes match; focus on scenarios 1-3 (Perish Song, Explosion, Explosion vs Ghost), 10 (Thick Club overflow), 11 (Badge boost stacking)

Verify Thick Club overflow scenario: Marowak Lv100 with Thick Club + Swords Dance (+6 Attack), high Attack base — confirm the damage calculation produces the wrap-around low value, not the expected high value
Test damage variation range: run 10,000 damage calculations with fixed seed and verify all variation results fall in [85%, 100%] of pre-variation damage

Verify type effectiveness chain: test a Water move vs Fire/Rock dual-type (should be 2x total = 2x for Fire super-effective, 0.5x for Rock not-very-effective, net 1x); test Ice vs Dragon/Flying (4x); test Normal vs Ghost (0x)

Test critical hit + screen interaction: a critical hit against a Reflected target should ignore Reflect (as if no screen) when defensive stage >= offensive stage; test both bypass and non-bypass cases

Test STAB + type effectiveness stacking: a Water-type using Surf (Water) against a Fire/Ground dual-type: STAB=1.5x, super-effective vs Fire=2x, super-effective vs Ground=2x, total modifier should be ~6x (1.5*2*2)
Verify 1/256 miss bug: with 100% accuracy move and all random values tested, confirm exactly 1 in 256 calls produces a miss (random byte = 255 causes the check to fail)

source_data.damage_formula.md:
## Integer Overflow Edge Cases
1. Thick Club + stat boosts: Attack doubled, then Swords Dance. If 16-bit Attack > 1024 before TruncateHL_BC, wrap-around.
2. Light Ball + stat boosts: Same for Pikachu SpAtk.
3. Reflect/Light Screen + high Defense/SpDef: Doubling >1024 causes same truncation bug.
4. Metal Powder: Adding 50% can overflow, making Ditto take MORE damage.
5. Critical hit: 2x multiplier can overflow 16-bit, capped at $FFFF.
6. Damage cap: 997+2=999 max; overflow before cap is stat-related and happens pre-cap.

## Phase 3: Critical hit stat bypass
If target defensive stage >= attacker offensive stage: ALL stage modifiers ignored, burn halving ignored, Reflect/Light Screen ignored. Raw base stats used.
If attacker offensive stage > target: stages applied normally (attacker keeps boosts, defender keeps drops).
source_data.battle_scenarios.md:
## Scenario 2: Explosion/Self-Destruct KOs Last Enemy
User HP set to 0 before damage. If target also faints, player wins.
Defense halving happens before damage. User considered fainted even if move misses.

## Scenario 3: Self-Destruct vs Ghost
User HP=0, defense halved, but 0 damage (Normal->Ghost immune). User faints for nothing.
Defense halving still occurs despite type immunity.

## Scenario 10: Thick Club + Critical Hit Overflow
Thick Club doubles Attack, then crit 2x damage. Can overflow 16-bit intermediate, wrapping to tiny value.
ASM: sla/rl doubles via sla/rl. TruncateHL_BC checks h or b (two-pass in single player).

## Scenario 11: Badge Boost Stacking Glitch
Badge boost modifies wBattleMonAttack directly. Stats not reset between applications in certain edge cases.
DoBadgeTypeBoosts in misc.asm modifies battle mon stats directly.

## Scenario 16: Counter vs Mirror Coat Type Check
Counter works against physical types (< SPECIAL threshold). Mirror Coat against special (>= SPECIAL).
Type of move determines physical/special: Normal/Fighting/Flying/Poison/Ground/Rock/Bug/Ghost/Steel = physical.
source_data.bugs_and_glitches.md:
### Bug 2: Thick Club / Light Ball Stat Overflow
Cubone/Marowak Thick Club or Pikachu Light Ball: Attack doubled via sla l; rl h.
If stat > 1024, wraps around to tiny value. Triggered by Swords Dance + Thick Club at high levels.

### Bug 3: Metal Powder Defense Overflow
Adds 50% to Defense. If >1024, same wrap-around, can INCREASE damage taken.

### Bug 4: Reflect / Light Screen Defense Overflow
Doubles Def/SpDef. In link battles, overflow fix disabled for Gold/Silver compat, so wrapping occurs.
Single-player has partial fix.

unstructured_notes: For the Thick Club overflow test: a Marowak at Lv100 with max Attack DVs/StatExp and +6 Swords Dance has base Attack ~657 (approx). Thick Club doubles to ~1314 which is > 1024. TruncateHL_BC processes this 16-bit value by right-shifting both Attack and Defense until Attack fits in 8 bits. If Attack=1314 and Defense is, say, 100: they get shifted right 2 bits each until both fit. 1314>>2=328, >>2=82 (fits in 8 bits at 82). Defense 100>>2=25. So damage calc uses 82 attack vs 25 defense instead of 1314 vs 100, which dramatically reduces damage. This is the authentic bug. The counter/mirror coat type boundary is at a specific type constant value — types below SPECIAL threshold are physical. In Gen 2 the split is: types with ID < 8 are physical (Normal=0, Fighting=1, Flying=2, Poison=3, Ground=4, Rock=5, Bug=6, Ghost=7, Steel=8 is debated) and types >= some threshold are special. Consult type_chart.md for exact type ID assignments.

goal: Implement battle state machine — top-level flow (InitBattle -> DoBattle -> BattleTurn loop -> cleanup), per-turn reset of flags
abstract_tasks: Implement top-level battle flow as a state machine: InitBattle (set up battle struct, load Pokemon data, determine battle type) -> DoBattle (enter main loop) -> BattleTurn loop -> CleanUpBattleRAM -> GiveExperiencePoints -> return

Implement BattleTurn main loop: each iteration runs HandleBerserkGene, UpdateBattleMonInParty, AIChooseMove, CheckPlayerLockedIn, BattleMenu, ParsePlayerAction, EnemyTriesToFlee (wild only), DetermineMoveOrder, Execute turns, HandleBetweenTurnEffects

Implement per-turn flag reset at start of each loop iteration: zero out wPlayerIsSwitching, wEnemyIsSwitching, wBattleHasJustStarted, wPlayerJustGotFrozen, wEnemyJustGotFrozen, and wCurDamage (2 bytes)

Implement CleanUpBattleRAM: reset all stat stages to BASE_STAT_LEVEL (7), clear all substatus flags, reset toxic counter, zero wBattleMode/wBattleType, reset flee counter, clear all wPlayerSubStatus through wEnemyFuryCutterCount
Implement wBattleType enum: BATTLETYPE_NORMAL=0, CANLOSE=1, DEBUG=2, TUTORIAL=3, FISH=4, ROAMING=5, CONTEST=6, TREE=8, TRAP=9, CELEBI=11, SUICUNE=12

Implement faint check ordering based on who moved first: if player moved first, CheckFaint_PlayerThenEnemy; if enemy moved first, CheckFaint_EnemyThenPlayer; this determines winner on simultaneous KOs
Implement battle exit conditions: player wins (all enemy Pokemon fainted), player loses (all player Pokemon fainted), wild flee, player runs, caught

source_data.state_machine.md:
## Top-Level Battle Flow
InitBattle -> DoBattle -> BattleTurn (main loop) ->
  [per turn]: HandleBerserkGene, UpdateBattleMonInParty, AIChooseMove,
  CheckPlayerLockedIn, BattleMenu, ParsePlayerAction, EnemyTriesToFlee (wild),
  DetermineMoveOrder, Execute turns (Battle_PlayerFirst or Battle_EnemyFirst),
  HandleBetweenTurnEffects -> [loop or exit]
  -> CleanUpBattleRAM -> GiveExperiencePoints -> Return to overworld
Source: engine/battle/core.asm lines 1-230

## Per-Turn Reset (BattleTurn.loop)
At START of each turn, zeroed:
- wPlayerIsSwitching, wEnemyIsSwitching
- wBattleHasJustStarted, wPlayerJustGotFrozen, wEnemyJustGotFrozen
- wCurDamage (2 bytes)
Source: engine/battle/core.asm lines 166-173

## Faint Check Order
Based on hSerialConnectionStatus: if player moved first: CheckFaint_PlayerThenEnemy.
If enemy moved first: CheckFaint_EnemyThenPlayer.
Critical for simultaneous KOs — whoever checked first and found fainted loses.
Source: engine/battle/core.asm lines 298-340

## CleanUpBattleRAM (what resets between battles)
- ALL stat stages reset to 0/neutral
- ALL substatus flags cleared
- Toxic counter, wBattleMode, wBattleType zeroed
- wOtherTrainerClass, flee counter, menu cursors cleared
- Large block zeroed: all wPlayerSubStatus1 through wEnemyFuryCutterCount
Source: engine/battle/core.asm lines 8288-8318
source_data.battle_mechanics.md:
## Battle Turn Structure
1. Player Input Phase: select Fight/Item/Switch/Run; Encored=forced last move; locked-in=skip selection
2. Enemy AI Phase: AI selects move or switch
3. Move Order Determination (DetermineMoveOrder)
4. Execute Moves (per battler): Pre-Move Checks (CheckTurn) then move effect script
5. Between-Turn Effects (HandleBetweenTurnEffects)
6. Residual Damage (ResidualDamage) called after each individual turn

## What Persists vs Resets
Persists within battle: stat stages, volatile statuses (confusion, encore, etc.), substatus flags,
  toxic counter, Future Sight counter/damage, last move used, Fury Cutter/Rollout counter
Resets each turn: wCurDamage, wPlayerIsSwitching, wEnemyIsSwitching, wAttackMissed,
  wEffectFailed, Protect/Endure (cleared by EndOpponentProtectEndureDestinyBond),
  Destiny Bond (cleared at start of user turn AND after opponent move), Flinch (in CheckTurn)
Persists in party (between battles): HP, PP, status, exp, stats, EVs, happiness, held items, Pokerus

unstructured_notes: The BattleTurn loop is the engine core — every battle interaction passes through it. The order within the loop matters: BerserkGene is handled BEFORE AI chooses, and AI chooses BEFORE player input check. CheckPlayerLockedIn checks for Rollout/Outrage/Bide/recharge (removes Fight menu options). The loop exits to different states depending on battle type (BATTLETYPE_CANLOSE allows losing without whiteout; BATTLETYPE_NORMAL triggers whiteout and money loss). UpdateBattleMonInParty syncs the wBattleMon data back to the party struct — this must happen before experience is given to ensure HP is accurate. GiveExperiencePoints runs after CleanUpBattleRAM, so all battle flags are clear when XP is awarded. The faint check order is significant: in a Perish Song scenario where both Pokemon reach 0 HP simultaneously, the check order determines the winner. This sprint is the backbone that all subsequent battle sprints build upon.

goal: Implement turn order determination — switching priority, move priority check, Quick Claw, speed comparison, speed ties, Vital Throw special case
abstract_tasks: Implement switching priority: if one side uses Switch and the other uses a move, the SWITCHER goes first (attacks the incoming Pokemon); if BOTH sides switch (link battles only), use 50/50 coin flip for order

Implement move priority tiers from effects_priorities.asm: Protect/Detect/Endure = priority 3; Quick Attack/Mach Punch/ExtremeSpeed = priority 2; normal moves = priority 1; Counter/Mirror Coat/Roar/Whirlwind = priority 0; higher priority always goes first
Implement Vital Throw special case: hardcoded to priority 0 BEFORE the effect priority table is checked; check for VITAL_THROW effect first and return priority 0 immediately

Implement Quick Claw check for equal-priority moves: if holder has Quick Claw, generate random byte and compare to item's activation rate; if both sides have Quick Claw, each rolls independently; if neither Quick Claw activates, fall through to speed comparison
Implement speed comparison for equal-priority, no Quick Claw: compare effective Speed stats (after stat stage, paralysis reduction) as 16-bit values; higher speed goes first
Implement speed tie resolution: if speeds are equal after all checks, use BattleRandom for a 50/50 coin flip; player wins on one result, enemy wins on the other

Write integration tests covering: priority move always goes first vs non-priority move; equal priority with one paralyzed (lower speed goes second); Quick Claw activation rate (60 activations per 100 trials within expected range); speed tie is 50/50 over 1000 trials

source_data.battle_mechanics.md:
## Move Order Determination (DetermineMoveOrder)
Priority order (strict):
a) Switching priority: switcher goes first; both switch = random 50/50
b) Move priority check (CompareMovePriority):
   +1 priority: Mach Punch, Quick Attack, ExtremeSpeed
    0 priority: All regular moves
   -1 priority: Counter, Mirror Coat, Vital Throw
   Higher priority ALWAYS goes first
c) Quick Claw: if equal priority, Quick Claw holder gets random first-move chance
   Both have Quick Claw: each rolls independently; speed check if neither activates
d) Speed comparison: compare 16-bit Speed stats (includes stat stage, paralysis quartering)
e) Speed tie: random 50/50 (BattleRandom)

Note: Roar/Whirlwind are priority 0 in Gen 2 (NOT negative like later games)
source_data.state_machine.md:
## Move Order Determination (DetermineMoveOrder)
Priority system:
1. Switching vs moves: switcher goes first (link battle: both switch = coin flip)
2. Move priority: Protect/Endure(3) > Quick Attack/Mach Punch(2) > Normal(1) > Counter/Mirror Coat/Roar/Whirlwind(0) > Vital Throw(0 hardcoded)
3. Quick Claw: random chance to go first for holder at equal priority
4. Speed comparison: higher speed first
5. Speed tie: 50% coin flip (BattleRandom)

Vital Throw special case: cp VITAL_THROW returns a=0 immediately (hardcoded BEFORE table lookup).
Counter/Mirror Coat/Roar/Whirlwind: priority 0 = after all normal moves, still in priority system.
Source: engine/battle/core.asm lines 442-561, data/moves/effects_priorities.asm
source_data.move_details.md:
## Move Priority Table (data/moves/effects_priorities.asm)
Exceptions (non-default priority 1):
Protect/Detect: priority 3
Endure: priority 3
Quick Attack/Mach Punch/ExtremeSpeed: priority 2
Roar/Whirlwind (Force Switch): priority 0
Counter: priority 0
Mirror Coat: priority 0
[Vital Throw: hardcoded priority 0 in DetermineMoveOrder, checked before table]

Note: priority 0 goes LAST (after all priority 1), priority 2 goes before priority 1, priority 3 goes first.

unstructured_notes: The priority numbering in move_details.md and battle_mechanics.md have a discrepancy. move_details.md says priority 0 for Counter/Roar (meaning last), priority 2 for Quick Attack (fast), priority 3 for Protect (fastest). battle_mechanics.md says +1 for Quick Attack and -1 for Counter. The state_machine.md uses the same numbering as move_details.md (0=last, 3=fastest). Use state_machine.md and move_details.md as the authoritative source. Quick Claw activation: the item description says 'raises 1st strike ratio' — the actual ASM stores an activation rate that the random check compares against. Based on game testing, Quick Claw activates approximately 60% of the time (when both sides have equal priority moves). Confirm the exact activation rate from item_details.md. Speed comparison uses the in-battle effective Speed (after stat stages and paralysis) not the raw party stat — this means a paralyzed Pokemon with +6 Speed stage could still go first or last depending on the opponent. Roar/Whirlwind are priority 0 in Gen 2 (not negative like Gen 3+) — they go LAST among all moves but can still interact with the priority system. Depends on Sprints 19 (RNG for coin flip and Quick Claw), 20 (stat stages for speed comparison), 25 (battle state machine framework).
